{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38443830?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/fallintoplace", "id": 38443830, "login": "fallintoplace", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM4NDQzODMw", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/fallintoplace", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49983", "cvss": {"score": 5.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-863", "name": "Incorrect Authorization"}], "description": "## Summary

In Deno, environment access is gated by the `env` permission. You can deny it
with `--deny-env`, or restrict it to a specific allowlist with
`--allow-env=FOO,BAR`. The expectation is that a program running without `env`
permission cannot change `process.env`.

`process.loadEnvFile()` (the Node-compatible API for loading variables from a
`.env` file) does **not** honor this. It only checks that the program has
**read** permission for the dotenv file, then writes every key in that file
into the process environment — even when `env` access is denied.

In effect, **`--allow-read` plus a writable or attacker-controlled `.env` file
is enough to defeat `--deny-env`.**

## Am I affected?

You are potentially affected if **all** of the following are true:

1. You run Deno **v2.3.0 or newer**.
2. Your program (or any dependency it imports) calls `process.loadEnvFile()`
   from `node:process`.
3. You rely on Deno's permission model — specifically `--deny-env`, an
   `--allow-env=…` allowlist, or running without granting `env` — as a
   security boundary.
4. The `.env` path passed to `loadEnvFile()` can be controlled or modified by
   a less-trusted party (untrusted input, user-writable directory, third-party
   dependency, etc.) and is covered by your `--allow-read` grant.

If your program does not use `process.loadEnvFile()` at all, or if it already
grants full `env` access, this advisory does not change your risk.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00098, "percentile": 0.00967}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-16T19:04:57Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-49983"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-06-16T19:04:57Z", "references": ["https://github.com/denoland/deno/security/advisories/GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/denoland/deno/security-advisories/GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/denoland/deno", "summary": "Deno: process.loadEnvFile() bypasses env permission checks and mutates process.env with only read access", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-16T19:04:58Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-4c8g-jvcx-v4hv", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.8.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "deno"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 2.8.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Deno: process.loadEnvFile() bypasses env permission checks and mutates process.env with only read access
